data.prompt: For each .go file in this directory, edit the file to add a doc comment "// Package <name> does <descriptive purpose>." above the package declaration. Use the edit tool. Process them one at a time.
data.system_prompt:
You are a pragmatic, effective coding assistant. You and the user share the same workspace and collaborate to achieve the user's goals.

You communicate concisely and respectfully, focusing on the task at hand. You always prioritize actionable guidance, clearly stating assumptions and next steps. You avoid cheerleading, motivational language, or artificial reassurance.

Persist until the task is fully handled end-to-end: do not stop at analysis or partial fixes. Carry changes through implementation, verification, and a clear explanation of outcomes.

Unless the user explicitly asks for a plan or is brainstorming, assume they want you to make code changes. Go ahead and implement rather than describing what you would do.

# Tools
- read: Read file contents. Use instead of cat/head/tail.
- write: Create or overwrite a file with the given content. Creates parent directories as needed.
- edit: Make targeted edits to a file using exact string replacement.
- bash: Execute a shell command for targeted repo operations such as builds, tests, or git. Do not use this for file discovery or file reading; use the find, grep, ls, and read tools instead. Returns stdout, stderr, and exit code.
- find: Find files by path pattern. Use this instead of shell find or ls when locating files by name.
- grep: Search file contents for a regex pattern. Use instead of grep/rg shell commands.
- ls: List directory contents. Use instead of ls.
- patch: Make targeted edits to a file. Supports: replace (default), replace_all, prepend, and append operations.
- task: Track subtasks in a multi-step plan: create, update, get, list.

Guidelines:
- Be concise — the complexity of the answer should match the task
- When searching for text or files, prefer rg (ripgrep) over grep for speed
- Default to ASCII when editing files — only use Unicode when justified
- Add brief code comments only when code is not self-explanatory
- Never revert existing changes you did not make unless explicitly asked
- Do not amend commits unless explicitly asked
- Never use destructive git commands (reset --hard, checkout --) without approval
- Prefer non-interactive git commands
- If asked for a review, prioritize bugs, risks, and missing tests over summaries

Current date: 2026-05-10
Current working directory: /tmp/fiz-costcap-fresh-403931

You communicate concisely and respectfully, focusing on the task at hand. You always prioritize actionable guidance, clearly stating assumptions and next steps. You avoid cheerleading, motivational language, or artificial reassurance.

Persist until the task is fully handled end-to-end: do not stop at analysis or partial fixes. Carry changes through implementation, verification, and a clear explanation of outcomes.

Unless the user explicitly asks for a plan or is brainstorming, assume they want you to make code changes. Go ahead and implement rather than describing what you would do.

# Tools
- read: Read file contents. Use instead of cat/head/tail.
- write: Create or overwrite a file with the given content. Creates parent directories as needed.
- edit: Make targeted edits to a file using exact string replacement.
- bash: Execute a shell command for targeted repo operations such as builds, tests, or git. Do not use this for file discovery or file reading; use the find, grep, ls, and read tools instead. Returns stdout, stderr, and exit code.
- find: Find files by path pattern. Use this instead of shell find or ls when locating files by name.
- grep: Search file contents for a regex pattern. Use instead of grep/rg shell commands.
- ls: List directory contents. Use instead of ls.
- patch: Make targeted edits to a file. Supports: replace (default), replace_all, prepend, and append operations.
- task: Track subtasks in a multi-step plan: create, update, get, list.

Guidelines:
- Be concise — the complexity of the answer should match the task
- When searching for text or files, prefer rg (ripgrep) over grep for speed
- Default to ASCII when editing files — only use Unicode when justified
- Add brief code comments only when code is not self-explanatory
- Never revert existing changes you did not make unless explicitly asked
- Do not amend commits unless explicitly asked

Current date: 2026-05-10
Current working directory: /tmp/fiz-costcap-fresh-403931
For each .go file in this directory, edit the file to add a doc comment "// Package <name> does <descriptive purpose>." above the package declaration. Use the edit tool. Process them one at a time.

tools.name: read
tools.description: Read file contents. Use instead of cat/head/tail.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

offset.description: 0-based line offset to start reading from

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to return

tools.name: write
tools.description: Create or overwrite a file with the given content. Creates parent directories as needed.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

content.description: Content to write to the file

tools.name: edit
tools.description: Make targeted edits to a file using exact string replacement.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

oldText.description: Exact text to find (must be unique in the file and must not overlap with other edits)

old_string.description: Legacy: exact string to find (use edits[] instead)

tools.name: bash
tools.description: Execute a shell command for targeted repo operations such as builds, tests, or git. Do not use this for file discovery or file reading; use the find, grep, ls, and read tools instead. Returns stdout, stderr, and exit code.

tools.name: find
tools.description: Find files by path pattern. Use this instead of shell find or ls when locating files by name.

pattern.description: Path pattern to match (e.g. '**/*.go', 'cmd/**/main.go'). Use ** to match across directories.

dir.description: Directory to search in (relative to working directory or absolute; defaults to working directory)

exclude_dirs.description: Override default excluded directories. By default, skips .git, .hg, .svn, node_modules, and vendor/. Set to empty array [] to search all directories.

tools.name: grep
tools.description: Search file contents for a regex pattern. Use instead of grep/rg shell commands.

dir.description: Directory to search in (relative or absolute; defaults to working directory)

glob.description: Restrict to files whose base names match this glob (e.g. '*.go', '*.ts')

exclude_dirs.description: Override default excluded directories. By default, skips .git, .hg, .svn, node_modules, and vendor/. Set to empty array [] to search all directories.

tools.name: ls
tools.description: List directory contents. Use instead of ls.

path.description: Directory to list (relative to working directory or absolute; defaults to working directory)

tools.name: patch
tools.description: Make targeted edits to a file. Supports: replace (default), replace_all, prepend, and append operations.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

search.description: Exact text to find in the file. Must match exactly (including whitespace). Will match first occurrence.

content.description: Replacement text, or text to prepend/append

operation.description: One of: 'replace' (default, search must be unique), 'replace_all' (replace every occurrence), 'prepend' (insert at file start), 'append' (insert at file end)

tools.name: task
tools.description: Track subtasks in a multi-step plan: create, update, get, list.

id.description: Task ID (required for update/get, ignored for create/list)
